prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_CWT_catboost', seasonality_champion='NaiveSeasonal', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.92) and high seasonality variance (0.79), with different models dominating trend capture (ONLY_CWT_catboost) vs seasonality (NaiveSeasonal). The early/late horizon specialists differ significantly, confirming that no single model dominates across all forecast steps. These patterns strongly support DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths while mitigating individual model weaknesses.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_CWT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "NaiveSeasonal",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_CWT_catboost",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "rf", "late": "ARIMA"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_CWT_catboost", "CWT_catboost", "ARIMA", "FT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "FT_catboost", "CWT_svr", "DWT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["NaiveMovingAverage", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "FT_rf", "NaiveSeasonal", "ONLY_FT_rf", "catboost", "DWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.9166,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.92) and high seasonality variance (0.79), with different models dominating trend capture (ONLY_CWT_catboost) vs seasonality (NaiveSeasonal). The early/late horizon specialists differ significantly, confirming that no single model dominates across all forecast steps. These patterns strongly support DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths while mitigating individual model weaknesses."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": true,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=10 indicates high model diversity across horizons, supporting per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.3849 (≥0.25 threshold) confirms extreme disagreement requiring robust combination like dba_combination. Pattern analyst explicitly recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.79). RMSE spread_ratio=0.9166 justifies rmse_focus preset. topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 added as complementary selection strategy with moderate k."
}
raw.skeptic: {"add_names": [], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current candidate set is diverse (1 baseline: dba_combination, 2 selection: best_per_horizon_by_validation & topk_mean_per_horizon_k5, 1 weighted: inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No leakage detected - all candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection with n_windows=3. Top candidate is dba_combination (score=0.88277) which is a baseline and safe. Universe leaderboards show no non-baseline candidate that beats all current candidates. Candidate set has 4 different types, well within diversity limits (<4 of same type). No pruning needed.", "changes": [], "when_good": ""}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["robust_median", "baseline_mean"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current set has only weighted/selection methods (dba_combination, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5, best_per_horizon_by_validation, inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). Universe leaderboards show baseline_mean (RMSE=77.91) and robust_median (RMSE=78.51) outperform current candidates (~81-82 RMSE). With n_windows=3 (low), adding baselines provides stability anchor. relative_spread_mean=0.3849 is high disagreement, so keeping weighted methods is correct. Adding robust_median handles potential outliers; baseline_mean offers simple averaging alternative.", "changes": ["added robust_median for outlier robustness", "added baseline_mean as stable baseline anchor"], "when_good": "When n_windows >= 6 and relative_spread_mean < 0.25, consider removing some weighted methods in favor of ridge_stacking or simpler baselines."}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.92) and high seasonality variance (0.79), with different models dominating trend capture (ONLY_CWT_catboost) vs seasonality (NaiveSeasonal). The early/late horizon specialists differ significantly, confirming that no single model dominates across all forecast steps. These patterns strongly support DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths while mitigating individual model weaknesses.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=10 indicates high model diversity across horizons, supporting per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.3849 (≥0.25 threshold) confirms extreme disagreement requiring robust combination like dba_combination. Pattern analyst explicitly recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.79). RMSE spread_ratio=0.9166 justifies rmse_focus preset. topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 added as complementary selection strategy with moderate k.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set is diverse (1 baseline: dba_combination, 2 selection: best_per_horizon_by_validation & topk_mean_per_horizon_k5, 1 weighted: inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No leakage detected - all candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection with n_windows=3. Top candidate is dba_combination (score=0.88277) which is a baseline and safe. Universe leaderboards show no non-baseline candidate that beats all current candidates. Candidate set has 4 different types, well within diversity limits (<4 of same type). No pruning needed.

statistician.rationale: Current set has only weighted/selection methods (dba_combination, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5, best_per_horizon_by_validation, inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). Universe leaderboards show baseline_mean (RMSE=77.91) and robust_median (RMSE=78.51) outperform current candidates (~81-82 RMSE). With n_windows=3 (low), adding baselines provides stability anchor. relative_spread_mean=0.3849 is high disagreement, so keeping weighted methods is correct. Adding robust_median handles potential outliers; baseline_mean offers simple averaging alternative.
changes: added baseline_mean as stable baseline anchor
statistician.when_good: When n_windows >= 6 and relative_spread_mean < 0.25, consider removing some weighted methods in favor of ridge_stacking or simpler baselines.
